steps.title: Identify the given equation
steps.content: The given equation is $m = 6(n + p)$. The question asks which choice correctly gives $m$ in terms of $n$ and $p$ — that is, which matches the given equation.

steps.title: Compare each choice to the given equation
steps.content:
• Choice A — '$m = \\frac{6(n+p)}{11}$': Divides by 11, which is not in the original equation. ✗
  • Choice B — '$m = 6(n + p)$': Identical to the given equation. ✓
  • Choice C — '$m = 6(n + p) - 11$': Subtracts 11, which is not in the original equation. ✗
  • Choice D — '$m = 6 - n - p - 11$': Completely changes the structure; distributes incorrectly and subtracts extra terms. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
